test.name: Escalate to search-external-sites after FamilySearch census exhaustion
test.description: When a FamilySearch census search returns nil across 3+ name variants for an important plan item and the question remains open, the skill must invoke Skill('search-external-sites') to generate Ancestry/MyHeritage search URLs. FamilySearch's index has no phonetic fallback — a transcription error (e.g. 'Flynn' → 'Flinn') means no variant will ever surface that record on FamilySearch. Other sites fuzzy-match and can recover it, which is exactly why the escalation is triggered by the nil signal here, not planned upfront.

input.user_message: Execute pli_001 — search FamilySearch for Patrick Flynn in the 1850 US census.

judge_context: This is a POSITIVE test for the FamilySearch-exhaustion → external-sites escalation. After trying 3+ FamilySearch name variants for Patrick Flynn (all returning zero results), the skill MUST call Skill('search-external-sites').
REQUIRED: Skill('search-external-sites') must be called. Failing to escalate after FamilySearch is exhausted is a Correctness=1 failure — do not award partial credit for merely suggesting it.

The plan item pli_001 must NOT be marked 'completed' as if the search succeeded — it should remain in_progress or be logged as a nil result awaiting external follow-up, because the external search has not yet been run.
The nil log entry must be written (research_log_append called) with outcome 'negative', results_examined 0, and notes that FamilySearch variants were exhausted and external sites should be checked.
Acceptable: the skill may also mention research-plan as an alternative path. But it must escalate — suggestion alone without the Skill call is a Correctness=1 failure.

Tool Arguments: score 3 if primary searches include givenName on every call. Score 2 if a primary search drops givenName without having first tried givenName variants. Score 1 if multiple primary calls systematically omit givenName.
